channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Two powerful offshore earthquakes struck the same region in the southern Philippines hours apart on Friday with the first 7.4 magnitude temblor killing at least seven people, setting off landslides and prompting evacuations of coastal areas nearby because of a brief tsunam
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Officials investigate blast at Tennessee explosives plant that left 18 missing and feared dead
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — Officials were investigating a blast that leveled an explosives plant in rural Tennessee, as families of the 18 people missing and feared dead waited anxiously Saturday for answers.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Venezuelans in “Little Venezuela” — the largest home for the country's natives in the United States — are welcoming the news that opposition leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize with bittersweetness as deportation threats loom.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans is electing a new mayor and a wide field is pledging change after indictments
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans voters on Saturday are picking a new mayor to replace two-term incumbent LaToya Cantrell, whose indictment on corruption charges has left a wide field of candidates promising change to city hall.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pentagon will build a training facility for Qatari pilots in Idaho
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military is moving forward with plans to build a dedicated facility in Idaho to train pilots from Qatar, an important U.S. ally in the Middle East, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: President Donald Trump is in 'exceptional health,' his doctor says, after visit to Walter Reed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is in “exceptional health," his physician said Friday after he underwent a checkup that included lab tests and preventive health assessments at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's physical results released, got COVID shot ahead of Middle East trip
item.description: Trump, 79, got flu and Covid shots at his evaluation, which comes ahead of a visit to the Middle East Monday.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two jurors claim they were bullied into convicting Harvey Weinstein and regret it, his lawyers say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two jurors who voted in June to convict Harvey Weinstein of sexual assault said they regret the decision and only did so because others on the panel bullied them, the former movie mogul’s lawyers said in a newly public court filing.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: National Guard stands down in Illinois while troops patrol in Memphis with local police
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — National Guard troops patrolled in Tennessee's second-largest city for the first time Friday while soldiers in Illinois were engaged only in planning and training after a judge handed a loss to the Trump administration and blocked their deployment to the Chicago area.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cash App giving $100 to users through October. How to enter
item.description: CashApp is giving away $100 to thousands of users every Friday through Oct. 24, as part of its Cash App Pool sweepstakes.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TGI Fridays offering free food for some federal workers amid shutdown
item.description: The promotion will be available all day, every day during the two-week period, unless the shutdown ends before Oct. 23.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: What we know about the fraud case against New York attorney general Letitia James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James, a longtime political foe of President Donald Trump, was indicted by a federal grand jury Thursday on mortgage fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: BY PHILIP MARCELO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Many unresolved questions remain as a ceasefire begins in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Bombardment stopped and Israeli troops pulled back in Gaza on Friday under a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas. But will the agreement lead, as U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: White House to begin 'substantial' shutdown layoffs of federal workers
item.description: The White House said Friday it had started laying off federal workers, following through on a threat to do so amid an ongoing government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Connecticut man, woman charged with 31 counts of animal cruelty after dead animals found in trailer, bus
item.description: "That's crazy, man," Julius Bogues, who lives in Waterbury, said. "We ride by here and you never know what's going on right around you every day, very bizarre.
item.dc:creator: Jareliz Diaz, NewsNation

item.title: Family of man who died on Stardust Racers demands its closure after new reports of malfunction
item.description: The family of Kevin Rodriguez Zavala, the Epic Universe park visitor who died after riding the Stardust Racers roller coaster, issued a statement demanding that the ride be closed after new reports of restraint malfunctions.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: Some Palestinians pack up and move north toward their homes in Gaza after ceasefire goes into effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians displaced by the war in Gaza started walking north Friday toward homes they were forced to flee — or what remains of them — after the Israeli military announced a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas had gone into effect.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia: Attorneys argue smuggling charges should be thrown out for 'vindictive' prosecution
item.description: Attorneys for Kilmar Abrego Garcia were in court Friday morning arguing the human smuggling charges against their client should be dismissed for vindictive prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mayer, NewsNation

item.title: Timeline of the Palisades Fire manhunt: From alleged midnight spark to Florida arrest
item.description: Jonathan Rinderknecht, 29, was arrested on federal arson charges for allegedly starting the destructive Palisades Fire that began on New Year's Day in California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Virginia top cop hopeful torched by police organization over 'reckless' remarks about officer deaths
item.description: Jay Jones faces bipartisan backlash as Virginia police groups call his alleged violent text messages about House Speaker Todd Gilbert 'profoundly reckless' and disqualifying.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: The White House is responding bitterly to President Donald Trump being passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize despite efforts from Republicans and world leaders.
item.dc:creator: Mike Pesoli, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A history of President Trump's legal battles with New York Attorney General Letitia James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The day she was elected New York's attorney general, Letitia James blasted President Donald Trump as a “con man” and “carnival barker" and pledged to scrutinize his public policies and personal business dealings.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge blocks National Guard deployment in Illinois for 2 weeks
item.description: A judge on Thursday blocked the deployment of National Guard troops in the Chicago area for at least two weeks, finding no substantial evidence that a “danger of rebellion” is brewing in Illinois during Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO and SUDHIN THANAWALA, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tennessee teacher fired over Charlie Kirk posts files free speech lawsuit
item.description: A longtime Rutherford County teacher is suing her school district, claiming she was fired for what she shared online.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Milan, ABC 4

item.title: Travelers continue to endure delays amid government shutdown
item.description: On Thursday, Duffy told Fox Business that he may fire air traffic controllers who haven't shown up for work.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Last of 10 New Orleans jail escapees agrees to return to Louisiana after Atlanta capture
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans jail escapee Derrick Groves, who spent nearly five months on the run before being captured in Georgia, agreed to be returned to Louisiana in a Thursday court appearance.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More than 54,600 children younger than 5 may be acutely malnourished in Gaza, study finds
item.description: After two years of war and dire food shortages, more than 54,600 children younger than 5 in Gaza may be acutely malnourished, with more than 12,800 severely affected, according to a new study by a U.N. agency.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, Fox 5
